model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Illinois is the vastly superior team as a 3-seed with elite metrics (KenPom rank 7, Torvik rank 6) and the nation's #1 adjusted offensive efficiency, while Penn is a 14-seed with a KenPom rank of 159 and significant injury concerns. The Quakers will be without leading scorer Ethan Roberts (concussion) and may also miss TJ Power (illness), who scored 44 points in their Ivy League championship win. With ESPN Analytics giving Illinois a 97.3% win probability and a 25.5-point spread, the talent disparity and Penn's depleted roster make a 14-over-3 upset extremely unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois vs Penn Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois holds overwhelming advantages in team quality, offensive firepower, and health status against a Penn team dealing with critical injuries.
reasoningStep.evidence: Illinois ranks 7th in KenPom and 6th in Torvik with the #1 adjusted offensive efficiency nationally (131.2 adjO). Penn ranks 159th in KenPom with a 17-11 record and will be without leading scorer Ethan Roberts (16.9 ppg, concussion) while TJ Power (44 points in Ivy final) is questionable with illness. ESPN Analytics gives Illinois a 97.3% win probability with a 25.5-point spread, reflecting the massive talent gap in this 3/14 matchup.

result.text: Penn basketball's TJ Power may miss March Madness vs Illinois because of illness Skip to main content Home Sports High Schools Clemson USC Furman Paladins Greenville Drive Swamp Rabbits Fantasy Sports SAIL Swimming USA TODAY Sports Why Penn basketball might be without TJ Power vs Illinois in March Madness Todd Shanesy Greenville News Show Caption Hide Caption March Madness fans on why they love tournament time in Greenville NCAA Men’s March Madness Tournament 2026 practices open for fans to see Wednesday, March 18 at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville Penn's TJ Power, the Ivy League championship game hero, is questionable for the NCAA Tournament due to illness. The Quakers will also be without senior wing Ethan Roberts, who is out with a concussion. The status of Penn basketball’s TJ Power, hero of the Ivy League Tournament championship game, is in doubt for the Men's NCAA Tournament because of illness. Power is a 6-foot-9 junior forward who scored 44 points in an 88-84 win against Yale for a March Madness berth. He missed practice March 18 as the Quakers (18-11), No. 14 seed of the South Region, prepared to face No. 3 seed Illinois (24-8) in a first-round matchup on March 19 (9:35 p.m. ET) at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville. March Madness tickets for Greenville Regional in NCAA Tournament “We really won’t know until tomorrow,” Penn coach Fran McCaffrey said. “If you ask me, ‘Do you think he’s going to play?’ I’d say yes. But he didn’t practice today.” Power’s performance was the most points scored in an Ivy League Tournament and the third-most in Penn program history. He was named the event’s most outstanding player. Power was a unanimous All-Ivy first-team selection, the first team since Jordan Dingle in 2022-23. Penn and Illinois will wrap up four games May 19. Action begins at 12:15 p.m. with No. 8 seed Ohio State against No. 9 seed TCU, and No. 1 overall seed Duke (32-2) against No. 16 seed Siena (23-11) in the East Region. The evening session will begin with No. 6 seed North Carolina (24-8) against No. 11 seed VCU (27-7) at 6:50 p.m. East winners will go to Washington, D.C., and South winners will go to Houston for the Sweet 16. POSSIBLE FLAWS: CBS analyst impressed with Illinois basketball but also sees concerning results Power has scored 486 points and could become only the 16 th in program history with 500 points in a single season. Dingle was the last in 2022-23. Penn will already be without senior wing Ethan Roberts, who suffered a concussion in practice prior to the Ivy League championship game. It was his second concussion of the season. McCaffrey confirmed March 18 that Roberts is out against Illinois. Roberts was second team All-Ivy with 16.9 points per game. He was the national Mid-Major player of the week after scoring 30 points against Drexel. “When you’ve had your second concussion,” McCaffrey said, “that’s not something to mess with.” Todd Shanesy covers high school athletics for the Greenville News, Spartanburg
